title: Discord forwarding allows user-controlled mass mentions

evidence.quote:
const body = await req.json();
    const { category, title, description, wallet, timestamp } = body;

reasoning: The webhook message is assembled from user-controlled title, description, category, wallet, and timestamp. Discord interprets mentions in content, so a public feedback route can be used to send @everyone, @here, or role/user mentions into the configured Discord channel.
reproduction: POST a feedback body with title or description containing @everyone or a role mention. The route forwards that string as Discord webhook content.
recommendation: Disable mention parsing by including allowed_mentions with an empty parse array in the webhook payload, and consider escaping or normalizing markdown-sensitive fields before composing the message.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed, and there is no assertion on the webhook payload shape or mention suppression behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock fetch, POST feedback containing @everyone and <@&123>, and assert the Discord payload includes allowed_mentions: { parse: [] }.
minimumFixScope: Change the Discord webhook JSON body to include mention suppression and sanitize or escape user-controlled display fields.
